text.title: guardrails-implementation Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for guardrails-implementation as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Catching exceptions inside validators and returning True (is_safe) on failure.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A broken validator silently approves all requests, turning a safety component into a no-op. Failed checks must propagate as violations, not as passes.

failure-mode.description: Running LLM-based hallucination detection synchronously on every request regardless of stakes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Adds 200–800ms latency and doubles LLM cost per call. Reserve for high-stakes outputs where a context document is available; use async post-processing for review.

failure-mode.description: Trusting inter-agent messages implicitly and not running input guardrails on tool results.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Prompt injection can arrive through tool outputs, not just user messages. An agent that validates user input but trusts tool results is vulnerable to injection via poisoned tool responses.

failure-mode.description: Logging the full violating content including PII to a shared log store.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Audit logs must preserve evidence of the violation type without leaking the PII that triggered it. Hash or truncate violating content before writing to logs.

failure-mode.description: Retrying indefinitely on injection detection without a max-attempts block.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A genuinely adversarial user will send the same injection forever. Agents must cap retry attempts and escalate to human review or rate-limit the source.

failure-mode.description: Using regex-only PII detection without a purpose-built library like Microsoft Presidio.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Regex-only PII detection has unacceptably high false-negative rates for real-world content. Presidio combines NER models, regex, and context rules for production-grade accuracy.
